channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Some stuff left there, 15th June
item.description:
Been 2 days altleat

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Templar Drive, Lower Belvedere, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Black bin bags and kitchen stool dumped, 15th June
item.description:
Yet more fly-tppig at this location, still need a deterrent at this location, this is happening too regularly. I don't want to keep spending my lunch time reporting these issues.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Blandford Road, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned car, 15th June
item.description:
This vehicle has been abandoned and sitting in a free parking space for months. There’s grass growing around it. I couldn’t identify whether it was taxed as it’s a French registration. Parking is difficult in our area and due to the rise of many HMO’s in the area. We have to sometimes park 2 roads away. Please could this car be removed.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Marlborough Road, Widmore, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Post office rubbish, 15th June
item.description:
Rubbish on both sides of the street. Are there any council funds to keep this area looking better? It attracts all day drinkers

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Mount Pleasant Lane, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned car, 15th June
item.description:
This car has been sitting abandoned in a ‘free parking space’ for months. Tax ran out on 02/06/26. Mot ran out 12/06/26. 4 flat tyres and back bumper hanging off. In an area were parking is difficult due to so many HMO’s now on the road. Please could this be removed

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Marlborough Road, Widmore, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Opposite 17 Goddard Road, 15th June
item.description:
There is a rubbish box hidden behind the little shops that has been there for a few days now if somebody can please come and clean it up that’ll be great thank you

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Goddard Road, Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dog Bin, 15th June
item.description:
Email received by CSC inbox 14/06/26 18:11 • Please give brief details of your complaint or compliment below: The bins in Norman park are constantly overflowing which is unpleasant but also dangerous for children and dogs. Now the weather is warmer it will attract rats. • What action (if any) would you like us to take? Empty the bins more often than you are doing • Have you already spoken to somebody in the Council about this matter? no
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hayes Lane, Hayes, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly-tipping on public path, 15th June
item.description:
Fly-tipping on public path. BBQ coals from fire, wood pallet, black bins, misc rubbish

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Mare Street, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pedestrian crossing on the Little Green Street, 15th June
item.description:
Whenever it rains the east side of the lifted crossing gets filled with water which makes it very unpleasant for cyclists. It'd be great if the drainage can be added so the water doesn't accumulate there.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Little Green Street, Dartmouth Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti needs removing please, 15th June
item.description:
.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Court Farm Road, Mottingham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped sofa, 15th June

Dumped sofa

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 6, St Helens Road, West Heath, Greater London

item.title: Flytipping on pavement, 15th June
item.description:
Flytipping on pavement - looks like a load of cables/tubes/pipes

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Thomas Road, Barnes Cray, Greater London

item.title: Flytipping, 15th June
item.description:
Bags and old furniture fly tipped on the pavement
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Casimir Road, Hackney, London

item.title: Railings andotbwrfubbish dumpwd, 15th June
item.description:
Bar A Baris using this area to store their railings it unacceptable other rubbish is also being f dumped here

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Victorian Grove, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytip, 15th June
item.description:
Two large sofas and various other rubbish dumped by a tree on the Corner of Croft Close Chislehurst

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Croft Close, Elmstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Numerous bags of rubbish left on ground attracting vermin, 15th June
item.description:
This area around the communal bins on Arnold Estate is frequently left uncleaned with full rubbish bags not picked up by the estate cleaner. This is a persistent problem and no improvement despite reports back to cleaning supervisor. The area is attracting rats and foxes. The cleaning standards on Arnold Estate are a disgrace.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Sweeney Crescent, Bermondsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lots of rubbish bags left pilled up against lamppost, 15th June
item.description:
Rubbish bags have been piling up against the lamppost for a while and aren't being collected. It's not clear who is responsible for them but they are unsightly and are now taking up a large amount of pavement space where they were leaving out chairs and tables at one point
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 24,26, Great Suffolk Street, Southwark, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
